name: Focaccia Condita
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir the yeast into the warm water in a mixing bowl and let proof for 10 minutes.
2. Add the 3 cups of water and squeeze the biga into the flour through your fingers.
3. Whisk in the flour and the salt, about half a cup at a time, until the dough becomes too thick to whisk.
4. Using your hands, continue to mix until a dough is formed. Set it on a floured surface and knead for 13 to 15 minutes until smooth and elastic.
5. Set the dough in a large, lightly oiled bowl, cover tightly, and leave to rise in a cool spot until tripled, about 8 hours.
6. Shape the dough by turning out onto a floured surface and divide into 3 pieces. Shape each piece into a rough ball.
7. Oil three 14-inch or 15-inch pizza pans and stretch each ball of dough to cover (or roll with a rolling pin). If the dough resists, allow it to rest for a few minutes before resuming. You will need a 1-inch rim on each dough base. The dough can be left for up to an hour before being topped.
8. Sprinkle the oregano over the dough and drizzle with oil, spreading it evenly over the top.
9. Squeeze the tomato chunks right onto the dough and scatter the rest on top.
10. Add green onions and capers.
11. Finish by sprinkling salt over.
12. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Bake pizzas until the crusts are golden, about 20 to 25 minutes. Brush edges with oil and serve hot.
Variation: Shape the dough into three rounds and place on oiled baking sheets sprinkled with cornmeal. Cover and let rise for 2 hours. Bake at 450°F until hollow when tapped. Makes 3 loaves.
Carol Field, "Italy in Small Bites"
